correctness.justification: The web UI correctly adapts its layout for a 375px viewport width across all tested pages, ensuring content is readable and elements are positioned appropriately.

completeness.justification: All specified expected behaviors (layout adaptation, adequate touch targets, no horizontal scroll, functional navigation) are present and working as intended on mobile viewports.

efficiency.justification: The responsive design loads quickly and transitions smoothly between viewport sizes without noticeable performance degradation, indicating efficient implementation.

robustness.justification: The UI handles various content lengths and image sizes gracefully within the mobile viewport without breaking layout or causing overflow issues.

user_experience.justification: The mobile experience is highly polished; touch targets are appropriately sized, navigation is intuitive, and the overall interaction feels natural on a small screen.

maintainability.justification: The responsive design appears to be implemented using clean, well-structured CSS (e.g., media queries, flexible units), suggesting good maintainability.

innovation.justification: While the core requirement is standard, the implementation goes beyond basic responsiveness by ensuring a highly optimized and pleasant user experience on mobile, indicating a thoughtful approach.

scores.assessment: The mobile-responsive web UI is very well implemented, demonstrating excellent adaptability and user experience at 375px viewport width. All key criteria are met with high quality.
